channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Ben Horowitz’s dual support for Trump and Harris says a lot about the Valley’s AI politics
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. I’m Mark Sullivan, a senior writer at Fast Company, covering emerging tech, AI, and tech policy.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Marriott settles with the FTC for $52 million over data breaches
item.description: Marriott International has agreed to pay $52 million and make changes to bolster its data security to resolve state and federal claims related to major data breaches that affected more than 300 million of its customers worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Biometric data: Is it safe to hand it over to any company that asks?
item.description: Biometric data, like face and fingerprint scans, can help you save time when accessing your personal data – but with it comes a whole host of privacy risks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Spruce Pine Quartz Production Resumes After Hurricane Helene
item.description: Spruce Pine quartz production restarts following severe damage from Hurricane Helene, impacting the local economy and high-tech industries worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane’s impact on semiconductor industry remains a threat
item.description: One of two plants that mine high-purity quartz and silica sand needed for the production of semiconductors and other high-tech hardware has reopened operations after being shut down for more than a week.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: DoD considers faster acquisition pathway for AI
item.description: The Army is exploring the idea of creating a separate path or a sub-path within the software acquisition pathway specifically for AI. The post DoD considers faster acquisition pathway for AI first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: Only 'limited by your imagination': Gallium Nitride breakthrough could make LED displays more affordable and convert your smartphone screen into an antenna
item.description: Gallium Nitride breakthrough could make LED displays more affordable and convert your smartphone screen into an antenna
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Good news, gamers – AMD could launch a cheap Radeon GPU soon that may shake up the budget card market
item.description: Some good news for gamers, as AMD could be revealing a budget Radeon RX 7650 GRE GPU during CES 2025.
item.dc:creator: allisa.james@futurenet.com (Allisa James), TechRadar

item.title: Headspace Launches AI-Powered Chatbot Ebb for Personalized Mental Health Support
item.description: Headspace introduces Ebb, a generative AI-powered chatbot offering instant, personalized mental health support through life's ebbs and flows.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: The inside story of Roku’s TV program
item.description: Ten years ago, streaming stick maker Roku released its first smart TVs in partnership with Hisense and TCL, two Chinese TV manufacturers that few had heard of in the U.S. at that time. In the years since, Roku has become the domestic smart TV market leader.
item.dc:creator: Janko Roettgers, Fast Company

item.title: Solar Radiation Storm Hits Earth: What You Need to Know
item.description: A strong solar radiation storm event is hitting Earth, with a geomagnetic storm expected to follow. Find out what you need to know about the impacts on our planet.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Cisco revamps key DevNet sandboxes
item.description: Cisco has expanded its DevNet sandbox environments to make it easier for network professionals to test and develop applications for key enterprise networking technologies.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How a Cold War plan to stop nuclear proliferation could protect the world from an AI arms race
item.description: As Mark Twain said, “History doesn’t often repeat itself, but it does rhyme.” We are fortunate that it does, because it means we can use lessons from the past at least as a rough guide to the present.
item.dc:creator: Ash Jogalekar, Fast Company

item.title: This privacy and compliance threat throws shade at iPhone Mirroring
item.description: Apple-focused IT admins using device management software should perhaps temporarily disable iPhone Mirroring across their device fleets to prevent inadvertent privacy or compliance challenges as a result of using the new macOS Sequoia feature. (Apple is supposed to be working on a fix already.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI as a catalyst for ESG: Empowering CIOs to drive sustainable innovation
item.description: In today’s fast-evolving business landscape, environmental, social and governance (ESG) criteria have become fundamental to corporate responsibility and long-term success.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: El reto de gestionar las TI en educación pasa por la inteligencia artificial
item.description: Las distintas herramientas que se engloban dentro de la etiqueta de inteligencia artificial tienen una serie de características comunes, independientemente del campo en el que se apliquen: cuentan con un gran potencial de aprovechamiento pero implican a su vez una serie de retos, tanto desde la part
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 15 domande sulla trasformazione digitale alle quali ogni CIO deve rispondere
item.description: La trasformazione digitale (DX) [in inglese] rimane al centro dell’attenzione dei CIO, con molteplici analisi e studi che indicano come rimanga una delle priorità assolute per i leader IT anche alla luce di un decennio di iniziative in tal senso.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Wimbledon tennis tournament replaces human line judges with electronic system
item.description: Wimbledon is replacing line judges with electronic line-calling, the latest step into the modern age by the oldest Grand Slam tennis tournament.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: PMI, 프로젝트 성공의 기준 재정립한다 “11월 연구 결과 공개”
item.description: 글로벌 프로젝트 관리 기관 PMI(Project Management Institute)가 프로젝트 성공에 대한 정의와 프로젝트 전문가의 역할에 대한 기존 관념을 바꿀 새로운 연구를 11월 공개한다고 밝혔다. 이 조직의 새로운 정의에 따르면 ‘성공적인 프로젝트란 투입한 노력과 비용 대비 가치 있는 결과를 창출하는 것’이다. PMI는 프로젝트 성공의 개념을 새롭게 정립하는 야심 찬 시도라며, 앞으로 프로젝트 전문가들의 역할 인식에 근본적인 변화를 가져올 것으로 예상했다.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Tesla is unveiling its long-awaited robotaxi amid doubts about the technology it runs on
item.description: Expectations are high for Tesla’s long-awaited robotaxi unveiling at a Hollywood studio Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Tom Krisher, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Twin boys born conjoined celebrate 1st birthday after separation surgery
item.description: Twin brothers who were born conjoined recently celebrated their first birthday after undergoing successful separation surgery.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A new way to ensure government and industry have enough cybersecurity people
item.description: "We could view cybersecurity as an entry level problem, but it is probably more of an intermediate and advanced role problem," Keith Clement said. The post A new way to ensure government and industry have enough cybersecurity people first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Office vacancy levels soar to record highs in biggest Bay Area markets
item.description: The Bay Area's three primary office markets, haunted by empty buildings, have reached forbidding new milestones of record-high vacancy levels, according to a grim new report.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: American Water cyberattack renews focus on protecting critical infrastructure
item.description: CAMDEN, N.J. (AP) — A cyberattack continues to affect the largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States, renewing a focus on the importance of protecting critical infrastructure sites.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US considers asking court to break up Google as it weighs remedies in the antitrust case
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is considering asking a federal judge to break up Google after its ubiquitous search engine was declared an illegal monopoly, but it is just one of many possible remedies under review, according to a court filing.
item.dc:creator: SHAWN CHEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: DOJ suggests splitting off Chrome and Android to break Google’s monopoly
item.description: In a significant escalation of its antitrust battle with Google, the US Department of Justice (DOJ) has proposed splitting off Google’s Chrome browser and Android operating system as part of sweeping remedies aimed at curbing the tech giant’s “illegal monopoly” in online search and advertising.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Windows 11: A guide to the updates
item.description: A Windows launch isn’t the end a process — it’s really just the beginning. Microsoft continually works on improving Windows 11 by fixing bugs, releasing security patches, and occasionally adding new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: TikTok is designed to be addictive to kids and causes them harm, US states' lawsuits say
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia filed lawsuits against TikTok on Tuesday, saying that the popular short-form video app is designed to be addictive to kids and harms their mental health.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Former Google engineer and ‘Godfather of AI’ Geoffrey Hinton among Nobel Prize winners in physics
item.description: One of the most influential academics in artificial intelligence, Geoffrey E. Hinton, has been awarded the Nobel Prize in physics for training artificial neural networks and laying the foundations for today’s machine learning applications.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: American Water, largest water utility in US, dealing with cyberattack
item.description: The largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States announced Monday that it was the victim of a cyberattack, prompting the firm to pause billing to customers.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
